data.title: Netflix: Strengthening Engagement, Accelerating Ad Growth, and Strategic Warner Bros. Deal Underpin Buy Rating
data.description:
William Blair analyst Ralph Schackart has maintained their bullish stance on NFLX stock, giving a Buy rating today.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Ralph Schackart has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors that highlight Netflix’s strengthening operating momentum and long-term strategic positioning. He points to robust engagement trends, with a growing global subscriber base, improved churn, and record-high customer satisfaction, underpinned by increased viewing of high-profile original content. While non-original viewing was pressured by higher licensing costs following the WGA strike, he appears to view this as a transient headwind rather than a structural issue. In parallel, Netflix’s 2026 product roadmap—including interactive features like live voting, expanded gaming capabilities, and better discovery tools—is seen as a way to deepen user engagement and differentiate the platform.

Schackart also emphasizes the rapid scaling of Netflix’s advertising business, which more than doubled in 2025 to exceed $1.5 billion and is expected to double again in 2026, with AI-driven tools improving both ad planning and creative customization for advertisers. He views this ad-supported tier and the associated AI capabilities as an important incremental profit driver on top of the core subscription model. Additionally, the amended all-cash structure of the Warner Brothers acquisition, which maintains the $72 billion valuation while reducing leverage at Discovery Global and increasing transaction certainty, is framed as a strategically valuable move. In his view, the combination of rising engagement, accelerating ad revenue, product and AI innovation, and the content and scale benefits from the Warner Brothers deal supports a favorable risk/reward profile and justifies a Buy rating on NFLX.

In another report released today, TipRanks – Google also upgraded the stock to a Buy with a $98.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
